claim: Gent-Sint-Pieters to Paris has no direct service; one change is required, with average journey time about 2h07 and fastest 1h57.
quote: There are no direct trains running from Gent-Sint-Pieters to Paris, you'll usually need to make around 1 change during the journey. On average, it takes around 2 hours 7 minutes to travel from Gent-Sint-Pieters to Paris by train, the fastest services can get you there in as little as 1 hour 57 minutes.

claim: Gent-Sint-Pieters to Amsterdam Centraal requires one change with typical journey of 2h30 and fastest around 2h23.
quote: You'll need to make 1 change during the journey to Amsterdam-Centraal, as there currently aren't any direct services on this route. On average, it takes around 2 hours 30 minutes to travel from Gent-Sint-Pieters to Amsterdam-Centraal by train, the fastest services can get you there in as little as 2 hours 23 minutes.

claim: Thalys was rebranded as Eurostar in September 2023; former Thalys red trains now serve Brussels-Amsterdam-Cologne-Paris under the Eurostar brand.
quote: From April 2022 to September 2023, Thalys services were operated by the Eurostar Group, and since 29 September 2023, the services operate under the Eurostar name.

claim: Brussels-Midi to Paris by Eurostar takes 1h25 with fares from EUR29 standard, EUR55 plus, EUR115 premier, hourly; Ouigo Classique offers a 3h budget option from EUR10.
quote: Eurostar fares start at €29 in standard, €55 in plus or €115 in premier. Fares vary like air fares, so book ahead.

claim: Brussels-Midi to Amsterdam Centraal: Eurostar 1h50 every 1-2 hours with dynamic pricing from EUR25; Eurocity Direct (ECD) 2h08 hourly from EUR25 but terminates at Amsterdam Zuid not Centraal.
quote: Eurostar trains link Amsterdam Centraal & Brussels Midi every hour or two in 1h50. In contrast, 200 km/h Eurocity Direct (ECD) trains link Amsterdam Zuid & Brussels Midi every hour in around 2h08, only 18 minutes slower than Eurostar.

claim: Eurocity Direct is the new fast Brussels-Amsterdam train introduced as the non-Eurostar option, with no compulsory seat reservation.
quote: Eurocity Direct: new fast train between Brussels and Amsterdam

claim: Brussels-Midi to Cologne is served by ICE and Eurostar; fastest journey around 1h45 with through fares from EUR18.99 second class.
quote: Direct trains to Brussels from Cologne | SNCB International

claim: Brussels to Luxembourg is operated as a Belgian InterCity service taking about 3h16 hourly at fixed fares (EUR21.30 second / EUR37 first), unlimited availability; from Ghent the IC adds ~30 minutes.
quote: To Luxembourg by train | SNCB International

claim: Ghent to Lille has 14 daily direct trains (no change), fastest 1h08, typical 1h15 over 66 km, operated by SNCB.
quote: Yes, it is possible to travel from Ghent to Lille without having to change trains, with there being 14 direct trains from Ghent to Lille each day.

claim: Ghent to London via Brussels-Midi totals about 2h50 (Gent-Sint-Pieters to Brussels-Midi 30 min IC, then Eurostar 2h to St Pancras); through ticket bookable on eurostar.com lets you take any same-day SNCB train on the Belgian leg.
quote: The booking system will have planned your journey to give you enough time to catch your connecting train, and SNCB tickets allow you to take any train on your route on the same day anyway, so there's no stress if you miss your connection.

claim: Eurostar fares are cheapest about six months before departure (booking opens 6-8 months ahead); Tuesdays and Wednesdays are typically the cheapest travel days.
quote: Tickets are cheapest, on average, six months before departure. Customers who bought their tickets six months early last year saved £75 each way on the peak price.

claim: Eurostar Snap offers up to 50% off last-minute tickets for travel within 14 days; you book a morning (06:00-13:00) or evening (13:00-20:00) slot and Eurostar emails the exact train time at least 48 hours ahead. Non-refundable, non-exchangeable, 16+ only.
quote: You pick your travel dates and time slot, Eurostar picks your train times, and you get up to 50% off the price of your tickets. You can pick a date up to 14 days in the future, and you'll be told your train time at least 48 hours before you travel.

claim: The 'Any Belgian Station' add-on (which let Eurostar travellers continue from Brussels-Midi to any SNCB station for a small supplement) was abolished from 31 March 2025; tickets must now be bought to/from a specific Belgian station.
quote: The 'Any Belgian Station' product will be discontinued at the request of SNCB with the change taking effect from March 31, 2025. The final travel date is June 14.

claim: Since 15 December 2024 international tickets to/from Belgium are valid only at the specific named station; B-Europe sells Eurostar/TGV/ICE tickets with Non Flex / Mid Flex / High Flex tiers and lead times up to 11 months for EuroCity, ~6 months for ICE, 4 months for TGV INOUI and Trenitalia.
quote: international tickets from or to Belgium are only valid from or to the specific station you specified

claim: SNCB's Discovery Ticket (formerly B-Excursions) bundles a return train fare with entry to attractions (Pairi Daiza, Walibi, Plopsaland, museums) at a single discounted price.
quote: Discount on rail tickets: Discovery Ticket | SNCB-NMBS

claim: SNCB's Weekend Ticket gives 50% off any return journey within Belgium during the weekend window plus public holidays, applicable to a Ghent-based couple's domestic break.
quote: Weekend Ticket offers special tickets for any return journey (within Belgium) during weekend period (+ certain public holidays), with passengers receiving 50% discount from the standard fare.

claim: NS International is the Dutch alternative platform for Eurostar/ICE/IC bookings to/from Belgium and adds a per-person booking fee on certain reservations, useful when B-Europe sells out.
quote: Flexible booking of train tickets | NS International
